controller_design_notes: Same architecture as linear_tracker__baseline. After 1000 warmup batches, runs 500 batches of adversarial training against LinearDynamicsAdversary. The decoupling hypothesis predicts that the adversarial phase recruits u_ff(t) (the feedforward channel that warmup-only training left near zero) instead of inflating peak velocity through K alone.

resumption_strategy.mechanism: Full mid-run resumption via --resume; see linear_regulator__adversarial/run.json for details. Adversarial checkpoint cadence: every 100 batches.
resumption_strategy.rng_caveat: Adversarial sampling RNG is not explicitly checkpointed; resume is deterministic given seed+last_completed_batch but not bitwise identical to an uninterrupted run.

notes: Adversarial tracker run for the corrected 4-model Δv comparison. Pairs with linear_tracker__baseline. Δv_tracker = (0.280 - 0.158) / 0.158 = +0.776 ± 0.034 (SEM, n=5). u_ff utilization increased ~4x over baseline (|u_ff|_max 0.021 → 0.052) but is still small compared to K-driven feedback (K_max=4.78, typical |K·e| ~ 0.5). Discriminator prediction (Δv_tracker ≈ 0) NOT supported — adversarial training inflates the tracker's peak velocity nearly identically to the regulator's. See results/410d7ac/notes/decoupling_acid_test_mvp.md for the full discussion.
